resources:
RCT Education App
Select Language Login Register Create Account Register as a new patient Email Password Full Name Phone Number Welcome Next Submit Quiz Please answer all questions New User? Register Here Already have an account? Login Select your appointment below I Agree - Proceed Yes No Maybe Please answer all questions Login to continue
Counselling What to expect today? Today your dentist will examine your tooth and take an X-ray. The dentist will explain the full treatment plan to you. Number of visits needed Root canal treatment usually requires 2 to 3 visits. Each visit is important for the success of the treatment. Cost of treatment Your dentist will explain the cost of the treatment. Please ask any questions you have before proceeding. Proceed to Consent
Select your appointment below Appointment 1 — Diagnosis Appointment 2 — Root Canal Procedure Appointment 3 — Crown / Final Restoration Follow Up Visit Start Appointment 1 Start Appointment 2 Start Appointment 3 Start Follow Up Logout
Quiz Completed! ✅ Great job! You completed Quiz %1$d successfully. Appointment %1$d Completed! 🎉 You completed Appointment %1$d. Please proceed to the next one. Pending Appointment Reminder ⏰ You have not completed Appointment %1$d yet. Please open the app. RCT Assessment Reminder 🦷 Don\'t forget to complete your daily RCT assessment. Tap to open! Welcome Back! 👋 You have successfully logged in. Start your assessment! Registration Successful! 🎊 Welcome to RCT Education App. Please login to continue. Quiz Result Your Score Excellent! You got all answers correct! Good job! You got 2 out of 3 correct. Keep learning! You got 1 out of 3 correct. Don\'t worry! Review the education material and try again next time. Continue
Appointment 1 - Baseline Please answer these questions 1. Have you had a dental X-ray before? 2. Do you feel anxious about root canal treatment? 3. Have you heard about root canal treatment before? Never Once Multiple times Very anxious Slightly anxious Not anxious Yes No Heard a little
Appointment 2 - Baseline 1. Did you experience pain after the first visit? Yes No Mild discomfort 2. Did you take the prescribed medicines? Yes No Partially 3. Do you feel more confident about the procedure now? Yes No Somewhat
Appointment 3 - Baseline 1. How was your recovery after the root canal? Very good Good Painful 2. Did you follow the post-operative instructions? Yes No Partially 3. Are you ready for the crown placement today? Yes No Need more info
Follow Up - Baseline 1. How is your tooth feeling after the crown? Very comfortable Slightly sensitive Still painful 2. Are you brushing the treated tooth regularly? Yes No Sometimes 3. Did the education in this app help you? Yes, very helpful Somewhat helpful Not helpful
About Root Canal Treatment What is Root Canal Treatment? Root canal treatment is a dental procedure to remove infected pulp from inside the tooth. It relieves pain and saves the natural tooth from extraction. Why is it Needed? When bacteria infect the pulp inside the tooth, it causes severe pain and swelling. Root canal treatment removes this infection and prevents it from spreading. Is it Painful? Modern root canal treatment is performed under local anaesthesia. Most patients feel little or no pain during the procedure. Take Quiz
Root Canal Procedure What happens during the procedure? The dentist will numb the area with local anaesthesia. A small opening is made in the tooth to access the pulp chamber. The infected pulp is carefully removed. Cleaning and Shaping The root canals are cleaned, shaped and disinfected using special instruments and antiseptic solutions. This removes all bacteria from inside the tooth. Filling the Canal The cleaned canal is filled with a rubber-like material called gutta-percha. A temporary or permanent filling is placed to seal the tooth.
Crown Placement What is a Crown? A dental crown is a cap placed over a tooth to restore its shape, size, strength and appearance. After root canal treatment, a crown protects the treated tooth from fracture. Why is a Crown Needed? After root canal treatment the tooth becomes brittle. A crown provides full coverage protection and restores normal chewing function. Care After Crown Placement Brush and floss regularly around the crown. Avoid very hard or sticky foods. Visit your dentist for regular checkups.
Quiz - Appointment 1 1. What is removed during root canal treatment? The whole tooth The infected pulp The tooth enamel 2. Why is root canal treatment done? To whiten teeth To remove infection and save the tooth To straighten teeth 3. Is root canal treatment painful? Very painful Minimal discomfort with anaesthesia Extremely painful
Quiz - Appointment 2 1. What material is used to fill the root canal? Cement Gutta-percha Cotton 2. What does the dentist use to numb the area? Local anaesthesia Ice pack Painkillers 3. What is removed during root canal procedure? The whole tooth The infected pulp The tooth enamel
Quiz - Appointment 3 1. Why is a crown placed after root canal treatment? For appearance only To protect the brittle tooth It is not necessary 2. How should you care for your crown? Avoid brushing near it Brush and floss regularly Use only mouthwash 3. What foods should you avoid after crown placement? Soft foods Hard and sticky foods All foods
Digital Consent I understand that: • Root canal treatment involves removing infected pulp from the tooth.\n\n• The procedure requires local anaesthesia.\n\n• Multiple visits may be required to complete the treatment.\n\n• There are risks including post-operative pain and swelling.\n\n• I can ask questions at any time during treatment. I have read and agree to the above consent
Post-Operative Instructions Please follow these instructions carefully after your procedure: Avoid eating or drinking for at least 1 hour after the procedure. Take all prescribed medicines on time without skipping. Avoid chewing on the treated side until the final crown is placed. Some sensitivity or mild pain is normal. Contact your dentist if pain is severe. Maintain good oral hygiene — brush gently twice daily. Proceed to Appointment 3
Reinforcement Education Key Points to Remember Root canal treatment saves your natural tooth. Keeping your treated tooth healthy is important for long-term oral health. Daily Care Tips 1. Brush twice daily with fluoride toothpaste.\n2. Floss at least once a day.\n3. Avoid hard and sticky foods.\n4. Visit your dentist every 6 months.\n5. Report any pain or sensitivity immediately. Signs to Watch For Contact your dentist if you notice swelling, pain when biting, or if the crown feels loose. Early attention prevents bigger problems. Final Assessment
Final Assessment Last set of questions 1. How often should you visit the dentist? Every 6 months Only when in pain Once a year 2. What should you do if the crown feels loose? Ignore it Contact dentist immediately Use dental glue 3. Did this app improve your knowledge about RCT? Yes, a lot Somewhat Not much Complete Assessment
Anxiety Assessment Please answer honestly about how you feel 1. I feel nervous about my dental treatment 2. I am worried about pain during the procedure 3. I feel anxious about the number of appointments 4. I am concerned about the cost of treatment 5. I feel confident about my dentist Not at all Slightly Moderately Very much Anxiety score saved!
Satisfaction Survey Tell us about your experience 1. The information provided was clear 2. I understand what will happen during my procedure 3. I feel prepared for my treatment 4. The app was easy to use 5. I would recommend this app to others Strongly Disagree Disagree Neutral Agree Strongly Agree Satisfaction saved!
Your Procedure Information about your treatment No procedure assigned yet. Please contact your doctor. Category Description Your Group Intervention Group - You will use this app for education Standard Care Group - Your doctor will explain your procedure Take Baseline Assessment Anxiety Assessment Satisfaction Survey 1 Week Follow Up
Procedure Information What to expect during your procedure? Your dentist will explain every step before beginning. Local anaesthesia will be given to ensure you feel no pain during the procedure. Duration and aftercare The procedure typically takes 45–90 minutes. You may feel mild sensitivity afterward. Take prescribed medicines on time and avoid hard food. 1 Week Follow Up Please answer based on your recovery 1. How is your tooth feeling after 1 week? No pain at all Mild sensitivity Still painful 2. Did you complete your medicines? Yes, fully Partially No 3. Are you satisfied with the treatment? Very satisfied Somewhat satisfied Not satisfied Follow up complete! Thank you. -
1 Week Follow Up How are you feeling after your procedure? 1. How is your tooth feeling? 2. Did you follow the post-op instructions? 3. Did you experience any complications? 4. Are you satisfied with your treatment so far? 5. Would you recommend this treatment to others? Follow up saved successfully!
